summary: Solid all‑around show with standout Brent Mydland moments and tight first‑set variety, captured on a high‑quality audience recording that benefits from a slight –2 % repitch.
review: The performance is praised for its diverse first set, strong Brent Mydland contributions (Half‑Step Mississippi, Dear Mr. Fantasy, Lovelight) and several crowd‑pleasers like Sugaree, Estimated Prophet and Eyes of the World.  The audience recording (AUD) is considered the best of the available sources – clear and lively after a modest –2 % pitch correction.  Overall the show ranks among the top Dead concerts of the year despite a few average bookends.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently positive 4‑star reviews highlight strong musical performance and a good‑to‑excellent audience recording; the weighting favours the show quality, yielding a rating above four.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience (AUD) source praised as the best of three available tapes; clear and lively but runs slightly fast and benefits from a –2 % repitch to correct pitch.

standout_songs: I Need a Miracle > Dear Mr. Fantasy
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set praised for variety and tight playing; second set maintains energy with strong jams, though the bookends and pseudo‑encore are viewed as merely average.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers note Jerry was 'on' – delivering solid solos (e.g., Estimated Prophet) and keeping the band in fine form, despite an unusual signal‑effect change on Black Peter.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir's vocal performance is highlighted on Estimated Prophet where he "really squeals" and generally adds strong energy to the set.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland receives extensive praise for his keyboard work (Half-Step Mississippi, Dear Mr. Fantasy, Lovelight) and a guest appearance by Bruce Hornsby on accordion/keys is noted as a treat.
